intro: Carapichea galbaoensis, also known as Galbao, is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae, native to the Amazon rainforest. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include the treatment of fever, inflammation, and as an antimicrobial agent to combat bacterial and fungal infections. The bioactive constituents of Carapichea galbaoensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from Carapichea galbaoensis include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of various diseases.
